patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) has false-negatived on every hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap in the sub-0.02 band this month: KC@BAL 7/10 (+0.018, BAL won), BAL@KC 7/12 (+0.0189, BAL won), MIA@MIL 7/18 (+0.0185, MIL won), TB@BOS & MIA@MIL 7/19 (+0.0117/+0.0185, both won). The proposed >=0.02 threshold fix has not shipped. Both CHW@TEX (gap=0.0077) and STL@LAA (gap=0.0079) fall in this band today with pre-cap score 5/grade A, so the cap's math is suspect on both -- but I'm only pushing back on CHW@TEX, where deGrom starting reduces reliance on the exact bullpen exposure the cap penalizes; STL@LAA stacks a bullpen_fatigue flag plus a 15.06pp edge near the outer band with no equivalent offsetting factor, so I'm leaving that one suppressed.

Corrected a mis-application from this morning's earlier claude_picks run: BAL@BOS was CONFIRMed at GOLD there on the reasoning that SP-edge and PQI 'independently corroborate' the starred F1_xera_gap=1.39*. The operating brief is explicit that this is wrong -- SP-xERA edge is 'partially redundant rather than independent' from a starred (thin-sample) F1 signal, and the rule is to enforce 'no exception even when SP xERA gap looks large.' Only F3_swing_take_gap>=1000 counts as corroboration, and BAL@BOS doesn't have it. This exact starred-F1-no-F3 shape has now lost 4+ consecutive times in the archive (LAD@NYY, CHW@TOR both 7/19; earlier 7/12, 7/17). Downgrading to SKIP this run.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD prevention) shows a likely code-path gap: OAK@ARI reaches Claude as GOLD tier with edge_pp=-3.17 (negative) and no [HARD CAP 1] line in grade_reasons, while TB@TOR/NYM@MIL/WSH@COL all correctly show the cap firing on comparable negative edges today. Live statsapi check confirms ARI's home starter Mitch Bratt has only 2 career MLB starts (6.00 ERA) -- a genuine rookie-SP blindspot on the picked side, independent of the cap question. Two separate reasons to downgrade.

Verified today's full probable-pitcher slate against statsapi -- all 15 games match the diag CSV exactly, no late scratches. Ronel Blanco (MIA@HOU) is confirmed starting for HOU despite 0 Statcast pitches tracked this season; his career line (45 GS, 3.45 ERA) rules out a true rookie blindspot, so this is a fresh-return/rehab data gap, not inexperience -- the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO is still the right call given the current-season data hole.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0242, which sits above the 0.02 line where the sub-0.02 false-negative pattern lives -- this is a legitimate cap fire, not a misfire. CAP 10 also zeroes it as a marginal-favorite non-CONFIRM (pick_prob=0.534). Probable pitchers (Ryan/Bibee) confirmed via statsapi, no late news. Confirm SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 sub-0.02 false-negative band does not apply here (gap=0.024)
HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket

PIT @ NYY.reasoning: SP-xERA and PQI both oppose NYY (grade D, score -2), a clean dual-opposing SKIP. Archive confirms dual-opposing is suppression-only and splits near coin-flip directionally, so there's no basis to fade toward PIT either. Ashcraft/Weathers both confirmed as probables. Straightforward CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: dual-opposing suppression-only, not a fade signal

TB @ TOR.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 correctly fired on the -5.16pp edge (score 3->1), then CAP 10 zeroed it further. Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.25 (Cease F5-dominant, close full-game), adding real uncertainty about whether the late-leverage thesis holds. No qualitative override warranted. Confirm SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 negative-edge GOLD prevention (correctly applied here)

BAL @ BOS.reasoning: F1_xera_gap=1.39* is starred (thin-sample SP data) with no F3_swing_take_gap corroboration. The brief is explicit that SP-xERA edge does not count as independent corroboration for a starred F1 -- 'no exception even when SP xERA gap looks large.' This exact shape has DOWNGRADE-validated 4+ times running, including two losses just yesterday (LAD@NYY, CHW@TOR). No hard cap catches it here because the raw numbers look strong on the surface -- that's the trap. Downgrading despite the A grade.
lessons_applied: starred F1 without F3 corroboration = DOWNGRADE (4+ consecutive validations, 7/12-7/19)

LAD @ PHI.reasoning: SP-xERA (0.69 against) and PQI (+14.4 against) both oppose LAD, grade D score -2 -- a clean dual-opposing SKIP. Sheehan/Sánchez confirmed as probables, no late lineup news to weigh. Confirm SKIP per the suppression-only dual-opposing rule.
lessons_applied: dual-opposing suppression-only, not a fade signal

SD @ ATL.reasoning: Edge -8.31pp sits outside the [4,15]pp acceptable band and PQI leans slightly against the pick. CAP 10 already zeroes the marginal-favorite pick_prob=0.508 without a CONFIRM. No live signal strong enough to override a coin-flip game. Confirm SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket marginal favorite

SF @ KC.reasoning: Already demoted PLATINUM->GOLD by the rolling-benchmark guardrail (archive PLATINUM hit rate ~43%), then CAP 10 zeroed the marginal-favorite score to 0. Stage1/2 disagree by 0.27 (Wacha F5-heavy lean vs a near-even full game), adding uncertainty on top of the caps. Nothing qualitative changes this. Downgrading tier to SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket marginal favorite
PLATINUM guardrail demotion (~43% archive hit rate)

NYM @ MIL.reasoning: CAP 1 correctly fired on the -3.19pp edge, capping a real pre-cap A- (score 4) stack down to score 1 -- this is the cap working as designed, unlike OAK@ARI below where the same cap appears bypassed. acute_roster stress flag adds independent, unverifiable caution on MIL's roster today. Downgrading tier to SKIP; not stake-worthy despite the strong underlying SP-edge/PQI numbers.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 negative-edge GOLD prevention (correctly applied here)
unverified stress-warning caution

DET @ CHC.reasoning: SP-xERA (1.18 against) and PQI (-11.0 against) both oppose CHC, grade D score -2. Taillon/Flaherty both confirmed as probables. Clean dual-opposing SKIP with no live news to weigh against it. Confirm SKIP.
lessons_applied: dual-opposing suppression-only, not a fade signal

CHW @ TEX.reasoning: Overriding HARD CAP 7's suppression, not the team pick. Pre-cap score was 5/grade A (F-signal, SP-edge +2, PQI +14.9, Stage1/2 agree) before CAP 7 knocked it to 1 on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0077 -- inside the band that's produced false negatives on every occurrence this month (0.0077-0.0189 range, all wins so far). deGrom starting also reduces reliance on the bullpen exposure the cap is penalizing. deGrom/Fedde both confirmed as probables. Restoring GOLD conviction.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 sub-0.02 false-negative band override (5-for-5 misses if this holds, 7/10-7/19)

MIA @ HOU.reasoning: Confirmed via statsapi that Ronel Blanco is indeed today's HOU starter (career 45 GS / 3.45 ERA, so not a true rookie), but he has 0 Statcast pitches tracked this season -- likely a fresh return from injury/rehab -- correctly triggering the sp_savant_gate HARD_VETO regardless of career track record. CAP 10 also zeroes the marginal favorite. Confirm SKIP.
lessons_applied: thin-current-season-data HARD_VETO is a legitimate gate independent of career track record

WSH @ COL.reasoning: Triple-stacked suppression: CAP 1 fired on the -1.72pp edge (score 4->1), CAP 10 zeroed the marginal favorite (1->0), and independently F1_xera_gap=1.50* is starred with no F3 corroboration (only F2/F5 support it) -- the same shape DOWNGRADE-validated 4+ times. All three lines of reasoning agree. Downgrading tier to SKIP with high conviction in the suppression.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 negative-edge GOLD prevention
starred F1 without F3 corroboration
HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket marginal favorite

OAK @ ARI.reasoning: edge_pp=-3.17 is negative on a GOLD-tier pick but [HARD CAP 1] never fires in grade_reasons -- a likely code-path bypass (a negative-edge GOLD reaching me uncapped, matching the standing heuristic to flag and downgrade). Independently verified via statsapi that ARI's home SP Mitch Bratt has only 2 career MLB starts (6.00 ERA) -- a textbook rookie-SP blindspot on the picked team's own side. Two independent reasons to downgrade.
lessons_applied: negative-edge GOLD reaching Claude uncapped = likely HARD CAP 1 bypass, DOWNGRADE
rookie SP blindspot (<6 starts) on picked side, verified live

CIN @ SEA.reasoning: SP-xERA (0.89 against) and PQI (+10.3 against) both oppose CIN, grade D score -4, the slate's weakest stack; fair_prob 0.397 also sits below the 0.42 floor. Kirby/Abbott confirmed as probables. No qualitative reason to override a clean dual-opposing SKIP. Confirm.
lessons_applied: dual-opposing suppression-only, not a fade signal

STL @ LAA.reasoning: Pre-cap this was also a clean A (score 5) before HARD CAP 7 fired on a marginal +0.0079 bullpen gap, the same small magnitude that's been a false negative all month. Unlike CHW@TEX, there's no offsetting ace-innings-eater factor here, and this game stacks a bullpen_fatigue stress flag plus a 15.06pp edge sitting at the outer edge of the acceptable band -- two independent cautions with nothing to balance them. Downgrading tier to SKIP rather than extending the CAP 7 pushback here too.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 sub-0.02 band is suspect but not overridden here due to stacked bullpen_fatigue + large edge with no offsetting factor
